subject: Chemistry
title: Ricin: The Castor Bean Killer
description: One-minute skill: Ricin: The Castor Bean Killer.

hook.text: Castor oil is safe to drink. But the bean it comes from also contains ricin—one of the deadliest poisons known.

buildup.text: Ricin is a protein that enters cells and attacks ribosomes—the machinery that builds all other proteins.

discovery.text: One ricin molecule can destroy 1,500 ribosomes per minute. Without protein production, cells die.

twist.text: In 1978, a Bulgarian spy was assassinated with a ricin pellet fired from an umbrella on a London bridge.

climax.text: There is no antidote for ricin. Once it enters cells, the damage cannot be reversed.

punchline.text: One molecule shreds 1,500 ribosomes a minute. No antidote exists.

quiz.question: How does ricin kill cells?
options: It destroys ribosomes, stopping all protein production
It replaces DNA with non-functional copies
